channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Tech rout intensifies as selloff grips global stocks
item.description: Global stocks sold off on Tuesday, led by deep losses for tech stocks following a losing session for the sector on Wall Street.

item.title: Oracle shed 21,000 roles over the past year amid wave of AI layoffs from tech giants
item.description: "The adoption and deployment of AI technologies across our operations have resulted, and may continue to result, in reductions to our workforce," Oracle said.

item.title: U.S. issues sweeping Iran oil sanctions waivers, unlocking billions in revenue for Tehran
item.description: The U.S. issued a sweeping rollback of sanctions on Iranian oil, offering Tehran an economic lifeline as negotiations toward a peace deal proceed.

item.title: AI groups spend $20 million going head-to-head in New York race pitting Bores, Lasher, Schlossberg
item.description: Companies are spending big to shape AI policies at the federal level. And the Manhattan House Democratic primary will be a test of where voters stand.

item.title: Wall Street was once uber bullish on gold. Now it's changing its tune
item.description: The June Fed meeting set a hawkish tone. Now, several Wall Street firms don't believe gold will live up to their bullish targets.

item.title: Apollo curbs withdrawals after exit requests hit 17%, reigniting fears over private credit liquidity
item.description: Apollo is capping investor redemptions in its main retail-focused private credit fund after exit requests hit 17%.

item.title: U.S. fights with Brazil for China's giant soybean market
item.description: The U.S. Soybean Export Council makes claims to higher quality a selling point over Brazil's crop, as American farmers attempt to win back business from the world's largest importer

item.title: 'I like their money': Trump threatens lawsuits against ABC for reporting on Reflecting Pool
item.description: The latest action against ABC comes as the broadcaster faces two investigations from the Federal Communications Commission.

item.title: Tesla faces federal probe after Model 3 slams into Texas home, killing 76-year-old
item.description: Harris County authorities said that the driver, Michael Butler, said that he had been using Tesla's partially automated driving systems.

item.title: Ro Khanna challenges Elon Musk to televised debate after online DOGE battle
item.description: Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., has been a critic of the Elon-Musk-led Department of Government Efficiency cuts.

item.title: Brexit 10 years later: How the UK economy and politics changed, in charts
item.description: Ten years after the Brexit vote, CNBC compiled these charts show how the U.K. has changed across growth, immigration, sterling, trade and politics.

item.title: How Europe is grappling with extreme heat as red alerts issued in Britain, France, Spain and Italy
item.description: Several European countries issued red weather alerts as a fresh bout of extreme heat pushed temperatures beyond 40 degrees Celsius (104 degrees Fahrenheit).

item.title: Companies are demanding states cut red tape. Data center-wary voters may think differently
item.description: Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro touted a $20 billion economic development deal last June, the state's largest ever. It's caused Shapiro no shortage of grief.

item.title: 'We'll see' — Trump hedges on guarantee Iran won't use oil profits to rebuild military
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent authorized the import of Iranian oil and refined products into the U.S. through at least August.

item.title: How a $4 billion Indian startup won Meta's backing but lost its founder to WhatsApp
item.description: Indian fintech startup Cred will raise $900 million in a round led by Meta, but has lost its founder and CEO to WhatsApp.

item.title: Lucid to lay off roughly 18% of U.S. workforce, COO Marc Winterhoff leaves
item.description: Lucid Group is cutting its U.S. workforce by 18% as part of a cost-savings plan. It also said COO Marc Winterhoff is departing effective immediately.

item.title: Senate advances housing bill to limit private equity purchases of single-family homes
item.description: The House is also expected to advance a sprawling housing package this week that's aimed at creating more supply and making homes more affordable.

item.title: Childcare tax breaks can save families and businesses money — but few use them, congressional report finds
item.description: Childcare can be expensive for both parents and their employers. Yet existing tax incentives to help defray those costs may go unused, a new report finds.

item.title: 'Reward for failure': Investor support for Target Chair Brian Cornell falls to lowest level ever
item.description: Target Chair Brian Cornell built the retailer into a $100 billion-plus giant, but recent struggles and underperformance have fueled investor calls for change.

item.title: Amazon Prime Day arrives earlier this year. Here's why Wall Street is watching closely
item.description: This year could see a major drive to everyday essentials, a growing category for the e-commerce giant's annual shopping event.

item.title: Oil prices fall as investors continue to watch U.S.-Iran moves, Citi sees 'overhang' disappearing
item.description: Oil falls Tuesday as investors continue to assess the latest developments in the Middle East, while showing optimism the conflict may end soon.

item.title: Amazon Prime vs. Target Circle 360: Comparing membership benefits, cost and more
item.description: Depending on how often you shop with Amazon and Target, either (or both) of these memberships could be a no-brainer investment. Here’s how to decide.

item.title: SpaceX signs computing power deal with open-source AI startup Reflection worth up to $6.3 billion
item.description: SpaceX has turned its Colossus data center into a commercial computing power platform, landing recent deals with Anthropic, Google and Cursor.

item.title: China's 618 shopping festival growth slows sharply as consumer spending malaise persists
item.description: The latest consumer data reinforce how consumption in China remains subdued.

item.title: Singtel sells $773 million Thai energy stake as it ramps up AI and data center investments
item.description: Southeast Asia's largest telecom operator sold 2.8% stake for about S$1 billion in Gulf Development.

item.title: Beijing is summoning executives again, but here's why that's causing less worry than in 2021
item.description: Beijing is summoning tech executives for meetings as it did in 2021, but now it has less room to wield a heavy hand due to deflation and rivalry with the U.S.

item.title: Global M&A deal value on track to reach $4 trillion this year: PwC
item.description: Global deal value of M&A is on track to reach $4 trillion in 2026, making it the strongest year since 2021

item.title: Alphabet has its worst day in over a year on AI concerns after high-profile exits
item.description: Google parent Alphabet has seen consecutive high-profile artificial intelligence researchers leave.

item.title: CNBC Elite Advisors: Top ultra-high net worth wealth management firms for 2026
item.description: CNBC Elite Advisors recognizes wealth management firms serving high net worth and UHNW clients.

item.title: Nvidia's stock struggles as Kalshi traders bet chip prices are coming down
item.description: Shares of Nvidia have been faltering recently — and Kalshi traders predict that what the company can charge for chips is also declining.
